museums.louvre.desc: World's most visited museum, home to thousands of masterpieces.
museums.uffizi.desc: Florentine Renaissance treasure with Botticelli and Leonardo works.
museums.rijksmuseum.desc: Dutch jewel featuring Rembrandt, Vermeer, and Dutch Golden Age art.
museums.prado.desc: Unmatched collection of Goya, Velázquez, and Spanish classical painting.
museums.vatican.desc: Papal artistic treasure with the Sistine Chapel and Renaissance masterworks.
museums.nationalGalleryLondon.desc: British gallery with Turner, Constable, and Western art from the Renaissance.
museums.museeDOrsay.desc: Impressionism sanctuary housed in a transformed train station.
museums.hermitage.desc: Imperial Russian palace with world-class art collections.
museums.altePinakothek.desc: Munich's classical gallery with European masters from Renaissance to Baroque.
museums.belvedere.desc: Viennese palace featuring Klimt masterpiece and notable Austrian art.
museums.munchMuseum.desc: Dedicated to Norwegian Expressionist and his iconic 'The Scream'.
museums.mauritshuis.desc: Intimate Dutch museum housing Vermeer's jewel 'Girl with a Pearl Earring'.
museums.met.desc: New York colossus with two million works from all civilizations.
museums.nationalGalleryWashington.desc: American institution with Western art spanning from Medieval to modern.
museums.moma.desc: Temple of modern art in New York with Picasso, Pollock, and more.
museums.artInstituteChicago.desc: American museum with Asian, European, and American art collections.
museums.masp.desc: Latin American museum celebrating Brazilian and South American art.
museums.tokyoNational.desc: Japanese treasure with Asian art and Japan's national collection.
museums.palaceMuseum.desc: Imperial Chinese treasure housed within Beijing's Forbidden City.
museums.egyptianMuseum.desc: Guardian of pharaonic artifacts and treasures from ancient Egypt.
